original_hypothesis.title: BOLDUpgradeAction.perform Permissionless Entry Point With No Access Control Modifier
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: BOLDUpgradeAction.perform(validators) has no access control modifier, meaning anyone can call it. While downstream calls (ProxyAdmin.upgrade, IOldRollupAdmin.pause) should fail for non-authorized callers, the function performs complex multi-step operations including deploying new contracts (ChallengeManager proxy, RollupProxy) via CREATE2 with deterministic addresses. If a non-authorized caller's transaction partially succeeds before reverting, or if the BOLDUpgradeAction happens to have accumulated the necessary permissions, the upgrade could be triggered prematurely or by an unintended party.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Critical infrastructure upgrade functions should have explicit access control as defense-in-depth. The CREATE2 deployments use deterministic salts, meaning addresses can be predicted and potentially interacted with before the legitimate upgrade occurs.
deployment_assumptions: BOLDUpgradeAction is only called via UpgradeExecutor with proper authorization
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: BOLDUpgradeAction.sol: perform function has `external` visibility with no access modifier. Relies on downstream ProxyAdmin ownership checks.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function perform(address[] memory validators) external { ... } - no onlyOwner, no access control, no msg.sender check

preconditions: BOLDUpgradeAction has necessary permissions to interact with ProxyAdmins and old rollup
OR downstream calls don't properly validate caller authority

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: External function with no access control on critical upgrade action
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Anyone can trigger the upgrade if the BOLDUpgradeAction has accumulated the necessary permissions
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No msg.sender == L1_TIMELOCK or similar explicit check

analysis_questions: Does BOLDUpgradeAction ever hold proxy admin ownership directly?
Can downstream calls succeed from an unauthorized caller?
What happens if perform is called by a random address?
validation_requirements: Trace permission requirements for each downstream call in perform()
Test calling perform() from an unauthorized address
Verify all ProxyAdmin operations require correct ownership

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If the BOLDUpgradeAction contract holds necessary permissions (proxy admin ownership or similar), any external caller could trigger the full upgrade process including deploying a new rollup, potentially with attacker-chosen validator set, before the legitimate governance timeline.
prerequisites: Attacker awareness of the contract's existence

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Premature/unauthorized upgrade could disrupt rollup operations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Would require governance intervention to correct

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test verifying unauthorized callers are blocked from perform()

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: High gas costs for full upgrade action

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
